title: AI Wedding Invitation SaaS Platform — Full Build

requirements.text: Build a Motoko backend actor with the following data models and functions: (1) Invitation record containing id (unique slug like 'shivangi-avanish'), brideeName, groomName, weddingDate, weddingTime, venueName, venueAddress, googleMapsLink, familyDetails (text), invitationMessage, selectedTemplate (text), colorScheme, fontChoice, backgroundChoice, isPublished (bool), createdAt, updatedAt. (2) Event record containing id, invitationId, title, date, time, venue, description, eventType (Haldi/Mehndi/Sangeet/Wedding/Reception/Custom). (3) RSVPEntry record with id, invitationId, guestName, guestPhone, attending (bool), guestCount, message, submittedAt. (4) Expose CRUD functions: createInvitation, updateInvitation, getInvitationBySlug, getAllInvitations, publishInvitation, deleteInvitation. (5) Event CRUD: addEvent, updateEvent, deleteEvent, getEventsByInvitation. (6) RSVP: submitRSVP, getRSVPsByInvitation. (7) Photo gallery: addPhoto (stores base64 or URL string), deletePhoto, getPhotosByInvitation. (8) Music: setBackgroundMusic (stores base64 or URL string, autoPlay bool), getBackgroundMusic. (9) Slug uniqueness validation on creation. Store all data in stable vars for upgrade safety.
acceptanceCriteria: createInvitation returns a unique slug-based ID and persists all fields
getInvitationBySlug returns full invitation data including events, photos, and music for a given slug
publishInvitation toggles isPublished to true and makes the invitation publicly readable
submitRSVP stores guest response linked to invitation slug
addPhoto and setBackgroundMusic persist media references linked to invitation
All stable vars survive canister upgrades without data loss
Duplicate slug creation is rejected with a clear error

requirements.text: Build a multi-step invitation creation wizard (Step 1: Couple Details) as a full-page React form with fields: Bride Name, Groom Name, Couple Photo upload (drag & drop), Wedding Date, Wedding Time, Venue Name, Venue Address, Google Maps Link, Family Details (rich text area), and Invitation Message (rich text area). Show a live progress bar across steps. Persist form state across steps without data loss. On completion of all steps, call the backend createInvitation function and redirect to the creator dashboard for that invitation.

quotes: User enters: Bride Name, Groom Name, Couple Photos, Wedding Date & Time, Venue Details, Google Maps Link, Family Details, Invitation Message

acceptanceCriteria: All listed fields are present and validated before proceeding to next step
Couple photo can be dragged & dropped or selected via file picker and previewed inline
Progress bar accurately reflects current step
Form data is not lost when navigating between steps
Submitting the final step creates the invitation in the backend and navigates to the creator dashboard

requirements.text: Build Step 2 of the creation wizard: Multi-Event Management. Display a structured event creator UI where users can add multiple events. Each event card includes: Event Type selector (Haldi, Mehndi, Sangeet, Wedding Ceremony, Reception, Custom), Event Title input, Date picker, Time picker, Venue input, and Description textarea. Events are displayed as premium animated cards with gradient backgrounds, icons per event type, and smooth entrance animations. Users can add, edit, reorder, and delete events. Save all events linked to the current invitation via the backend addEvent/updateEvent/deleteEvent functions.

quotes: Add multiple events such as: Haldi, Mehndi, Sangeet, Wedding Ceremony, Reception
Display events as premium animated cards, not plain text

acceptanceCriteria: All six event types are selectable with distinct icons and color accents
Each event card shows all five data fields clearly
Adding a new event animates the card into the list
Events can be deleted with a confirmation prompt
All events are saved to the backend and reloaded correctly on revisit

requirements.text: Build a Template & Theme Selector panel in the creator dashboard. Offer at least 6 templates: 2 Royal Indian (ornate gold/red/maroon with floral motifs), 2 Modern Minimal (clean ivory/blush tones, geometric), and 2 Cinematic Dark (deep jewel tones, dramatic typography). Each template preview renders a live miniature of the guest invitation. Include color scheme picker (preset palettes + custom hex), font pairing selector (at least 4 options including Devanagari-inspired and serif options), and background texture/pattern selector. Switching templates must preserve all invitation data and only change visual presentation. Persist selected template and customization options in the backend updateInvitation call.

quotes: Users can switch templates without losing data

acceptanceCriteria: At least 6 distinct templates are available with live mini-previews
Switching templates updates the preview instantly without page reload
Color palette, font, and background changes are reflected in real time in the preview
All customization choices are saved to the backend
Invitation data (names, events, photos) is preserved across template switches

requirements.text: Build a Media Management section in the creator dashboard with two subsystems: (1) Photo Gallery — drag & drop upload area accepting multiple images simultaneously, with lazy-loaded thumbnail grid, elegant hover animations, image reordering, and individual delete. Images are stored via the backend addPhoto function. (2) Background Music — file upload for audio (MP3/WAV), toggle for auto-play on invitation open, and a preview player. Store music via backend setBackgroundMusic. Display a floating music controller button (play/pause/volume) in both the preview and guest invitation view.
acceptanceCriteria: Multiple images can be dropped simultaneously and appear in a lazy-loaded grid
Each photo has a delete button and hover zoom animation
Music file uploads and plays in the dashboard preview
Auto-play toggle is persisted and respected in guest view
Floating music controller is visible and functional in guest invitation view

requirements.text: Build the cinematic guest-facing invitation view at route /[slug] (e.g., /shivangi-avanish). This view is strictly read-only and non-editable. It must include: (1) Full-screen cinematic hero section with couple photo, names in elegant typography, and animated entrance. (2) Animated countdown timer to the wedding date. (3) Couple introduction section with invitation message and family details. (4) Premium event timeline — each event displayed as an animated card with icon, date, time, venue. (5) Photo gallery with lightbox support and smooth scroll reveal animations. (6) Venue section with address and a 'View on Google Maps' button linking to the provided Google Maps URL. (7) RSVP form collecting guest name, phone, attendance confirmation, guest count, and message — submits via backend submitRSVP. (8) WhatsApp share button that shares the invitation URL. (9) Floating background music controller if music is uploaded. The view must be publicly accessible via the slug without any authentication.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to /[slug] loads the guest view without any edit controls visible
Hero section displays couple photo and names with entrance animation
Countdown timer correctly counts down to the wedding date/time
All events are displayed as animated cards in chronological order
Photo gallery supports lightbox click-to-expand
Google Maps button opens the provided maps link in a new tab
RSVP form submits successfully and shows a confirmation message
WhatsApp share button opens WhatsApp with the invitation URL pre-filled
Page is accessible without login for any published invitation

requirements.text: Build a Creator/Admin Dashboard at route /dashboard and /dashboard/[slug] for managing individual invitations. The dashboard homepage lists all created invitations as cards showing couple names, wedding date, published status, and action buttons (Edit, Preview, Publish, Delete). The individual invitation editor at /dashboard/[slug] provides tabbed navigation across: Couple Details, Events, Template & Theme, Media, and Preview. The Preview tab renders the full guest invitation view in an iframe or embedded component to show live changes. Include a prominent 'Publish Invitation' button that calls the backend publishInvitation function and generates the shareable public link. No login or authentication is required for this initial version.

quotes: No complicated login system required initially

acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard homepage lists all invitations with status badges (Draft/Published)
Clicking Edit navigates to the tabbed editor for that invitation
All editor tabs (Couple Details, Events, Template, Media, Preview) are accessible and functional
Preview tab shows an accurate live render of the guest invitation
Publish button updates invitation status and displays the shareable public URL
Newly created invitations appear on the dashboard immediately

requirements.text: Apply an ultra-premium, luxury Indian wedding aesthetic consistently across the entire platform. Visual direction: rich gold, ivory, deep crimson, and blush rose as the primary palette; ornate but clean layout inspired by high-end Indian wedding brands; Cinzel or Cormorant Garamond for display headings, Inter for body text; subtle mandala or floral SVG motifs as decorative dividers; Apple-level micro-animations (smooth fade-ins, parallax scrolling on hero sections, staggered card entrances using CSS transitions or Framer Motion); cinematic full-bleed section transitions; glassmorphism cards for event and template selectors; mobile-first fully responsive grid layout. All interactions must feel premium — buttons have hover shimmer effects, form inputs have elegant focus states, and loading states use graceful skeleton screens.
acceptanceCriteria: Gold, ivory, crimson, and blush rose palette is applied consistently across all pages
Display headings use an elegant serif or Cinzel-style font
Decorative Indian motifs (mandala/floral SVGs) appear as section dividers
All cards and sections animate in smoothly on scroll
Buttons have shimmer/glow hover effects
Loading states use skeleton screens, not spinners alone
Layout is fully responsive and visually polished on mobile devices (375px and up)

requirements.text: Implement scalable URL routing for the SaaS multi-invitation architecture. Each invitation is accessible at /:slug (e.g., /shivangi-avanish) for the guest view. The creator dashboard is at /dashboard. Individual invitation editors are at /dashboard/:slug. The platform home page at / serves as a landing page showcasing the platform with a hero section, feature highlights, sample invitation previews, and a 'Create Your Invitation' CTA button. Ensure client-side routing handles all paths correctly and unknown slugs show a graceful 404 page.
acceptanceCriteria: / renders a polished platform landing page with CTA
/dashboard lists all invitations
/dashboard/:slug opens the invitation editor
/:slug loads the guest-facing invitation for published invitations
Unpublished invitations at /:slug show an appropriate 'Coming Soon' or 404 message
Unknown routes render a styled 404 page

requirements.text: Generate the following static image assets for use throughout the platform as decorative and branding elements, stored in frontend/public/assets/generated. Render these as img tags or CSS backgrounds where appropriate in the UI.
acceptanceCriteria: All generated images are referenced correctly from frontend/public/assets/generated
Hero section uses the cinematic background image
Mandala divider appears between major sections on the guest invitation view
Platform logo is displayed in the dashboard and landing page header

constraints: All backend logic must remain in a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo
No external authentication or login system in this initial version
Guest invitation view at /:slug must be strictly read-only with zero edit controls exposed
Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/ or frontend/src/components/ui
Media (photos, music) must be stored as base64 strings or URL references in the Motoko stable vars — no external storage service
All routing must be client-side React Router — no server-side routing
nonGoals: Payment processing or subscription billing (architecture should be ready but not implemented)
Internet Identity or any authentication system
Server-side rendering or SEO meta tag injection via a server
External map embed (only a link to Google Maps is required, not an embedded map iframe)
AI-generated invitation text or AI design suggestions

required: Cinematic full-bleed Indian wedding hero background with soft golden bokeh lights, flower petals, and deep crimson drapery — dark romantic atmosphere (hero-bg.dim_1920x1080.png)
Ornate gold mandala divider illustration on transparent background, fine line art style (mandala-divider.dim_1200x120.png)
Luxury platform logo mark — stylized intertwined rings with a subtle floral motif in gold on transparent background (logo-mark.dim_256x256.png)
Decorative corner flourish element in gold fine line art for use as invitation frame accents (corner-flourish.dim_256x256.png)
Abstract blush and gold watercolor texture for invitation card backgrounds (watercolor-texture.dim_1200x800.png)
